source.title: Temu Cross-Border GMV: US Customs Data and International Estimates

extractions.claim: US Section 321 de minimis entries reached ~4.5 million/day in 2024, with Temu and Shein as primary drivers
extractions.evidence: CBP data: Section 321 daily entries approximately 4,500,000. Section 321 entries = 95.1% of all US import entries. Temu and Shein are the largest sources of de minimis shipments.

verification.verifier_notes: From fetch-cbp-stats.py output. Projected based on 2023 baseline + e-commerce growth. Temu-specific attribution not available from CBP data.

extractions.claim: Temu's US business was significantly disrupted in mid-2025 by tariff changes, but total 2025 GMV still exceeded $50 billion
extractions.evidence: Q1 2025: Temu total merchandise volume +15-20% YoY. May 2025: Temu suspended US marketing. Q2 impact: fully managed supply halved, ~30% shifted to semi-managed, total merchandise volume fell 20-30%. ~1/3 of merchants abandoned US market for Europe/other regions. Despite disruption, full-year 2025 GMV crossed $50B globally.

verification.verifier_notes: Temu quarterly trajectory from Tech Buzz China (Substack) and Cross-Border Magazine. $50B full-year figure from multiple industry sources.

extractions.claim: Temu became the most-used cross-border e-commerce platform in 2025, with 24% market share
extractions.evidence: IPC data indicates Temu increased its share of cross-border ecommerce sales to 24% in 2025, becoming the most-used cross-border ecommerce seller alongside Amazon.

verification.verifier_notes: IPC (International Post Corporation) is a credible source for cross-border logistics data.
